University of Miami / Jackson Memorial Hospital
Internal Medicine Residency
Internal Medicine Resident Portal
Scheduling  •  Coverage

Welcome to the Internal Medicine Resident Portal

The Internal Medicine Resident Portal brings together the information residents use most throughout the academic year into one centralized location.

Rather than navigating multiple spreadsheets and documents, you can quickly access your master schedule, cross-coverage assignments, pull list responsibilities, conference coverage, and other scheduling resources from a single platform.

As we transition to our updated cross-coverage and pull list system — including conference coverage and gap coverage — the portal provides greater transparency into both what you are assigned and why.

🔍 Search Your Schedule
Type your name to view your schedule, pull list, cross-coverage, and coverage counts.
No matching resident found. Please check spelling or search by last name.

Disclaimer

This portal is for convenience and does not replace New Innovations or the Hub, which remain the official scheduling systems. Always verify there before making decisions. For urgent concerns, contact the on-call Chief Resident.

How to Use This Interface
A quick guide to each tab and how to get the most out of this interface.
📄 Official Pull List Guidelines (January 2026) The official program guidelines remain in effect. This interface supplements — never replaces — these guidelines and the Hub. Tap to open the full PDF. →

📚 Glossary

Every scheduling term used in this portal — defined clearly.

❓ FAQ

Quick answers to the most common scheduling questions.

🏥 XC Assignments

Full grid showing all residents across all periods. Search your name below the grid to see just your assignments and find swap partners.

📋 Pull List

See your pull assignments across all 26 periods. Jump to a period or search your name.

📊 My Counts

Your total pull days, cross-coverage days, and combined total for the year.

📅 Master Schedule

Your full rotation schedule with pull and XC markers. Export directly to your phone calendar.

🔄 Swaps & Requests

Submit swap requests, find the right chief to contact, and review swap rules.

Glossary of Terms
All terms and time definitions for the AY 2026–27 pull list and cross-coverage system.

Weekday Pull

Sun 7:00 PM → Fri 7:00 PM

Covering a team’s clinical needs from Sunday evening through Friday evening. You may be pulled for a maximum of 2 days during this window, day or night.

Maximum pull: 2 days

Weekend Pull

Fri 7:00 PM → Sun 7:00 PM

You cover Saturday and Sunday — a full weekend block.

Maximum pull: 2 days

Gap Weekday

Mon 7:00 AM → Thu 7:00 AM

A shorter mid-block weekday window. Covers Monday morning through Thursday morning.

Maximum pull: 2 days

Gap Weekend

Thu 7:00 AM → Sun 7:00 PM

Covers Thursday morning through Sunday evening. You are eligible for any 2 of the 4 days — not all 4.

Maximum pull: 2 days

Jackson Jeopardy (JJ)

Mon–Fri · 5-Day Block · PGY-3 Only

First-up resident for the team during JJ week. Day pulls only — no nights, no weekends.

You are first-up for the full weekday block \(5 days, Mon–Fri\)

Conference Coverage

Conference weeks only

Program provides 2 days × 2 conferences = 4 days total. Submit your conference coverage request 45 days in advance to Dr. Breno Bernardes.

Maximum pull: 2 days per conference

Balance Coverage

Fri 7:00 PM → Sun 7:00 PM

Same window as a weekend pull. Assigned to residents with scheduling deficits to keep everyone within ±2 days.

Maximum pull: 2 days

Cross-Coverage (XC)

Per 2-week period block

You work only 2 days total over the 2-week block, coordinated with your team senior.

2 days total per 2-week block
🏥 XC Coverage: Black, Gold & Silver

When assigned XC for a 2-week period, you work only 2 days total. You and the team senior coordinate:

⬛ Black

Work both days of one weekend (Sat & Sun). Off the other weekend entirely.

🟡 Gold

Off both days of one weekend. Work the other weekend in full (Sat & Sun).

⬜ Silver

Work 1 day each weekend — e.g., Saturday of week 1 and Saturday of week 2.

💡 Black & Gold together: One takes Black, the other takes Gold. Total = 2 days each. Always 2 days per 2-week period.
Frequently Asked Questions
Quick answers to common scheduling and coverage questions.
Why was the new pull list and cross-coverage system created? ▾

The new system reduces residents pulled on weekends coinciding with major conferences, encourages conference attendance, and streamlines coverage so PGY-2s and PGY-3s carry a more balanced load.

What counts toward my Total Coverage Count? ▾

Cross-Coverage, Pull List, Conference Coverage, Gap Coverage, and Balanced Coverage (BC).

How does cross-coverage work — do I work every weekend? ▾

No. When assigned XC for a 2-week period, you work only 2 days total. You and the team senior coordinate using Black, Gold, or Silver coverage.

How does conference coverage work? ▾

Residents may receive up to two conferences per academic year, with two days of coverage for each conference. Conference requests should be submitted at least 45 days in advance. See the Conference Coverage Request Form under the Swaps & Requests tab.

What is Balanced Coverage (BC)? ▾

Balanced Coverage (BC) is used when conference coverage cannot be assigned because of a resident's existing schedule. It provides an alternate coverage assignment to help maintain equitable coverage across the academic year.

Why do some residents have BC? ▾

Residents may receive up to two conference coverage weekends each academic year. If conference coverage cannot be assigned because of an existing schedule, BC may be assigned on another weekend to help maintain equitable coverage.

Why do some PGY-2s have 3 XC periods and others have 4? ▾

Based on call requirements for the year, four PGY-2s received 3 XC periods. These residents receive additional pull days to keep everyone within ±2 days total.

What is the difference between Balanced Coverage and a Weekend pull? ▾

Both cover Fri 7:00 PM → Sun 7:00 PM. Balanced Coverage is assigned to maintain equitable coverage across the academic year.

My schedule seems different from the general rule — why? ▾

Small variations may occur because of curriculum requirements, wellness considerations, coverage needs, or the existing master schedule. If you would like your schedule reviewed, please submit a question through the Questions & Concerns form under Swaps & Requests.

What about MedPeds residents' schedules? ▾

The MedPeds schedule was provided by the MedPeds chiefs. Any concerns should be directed to Dr. Daniel Schwartzbaum and Dr. Francisco Romeo.

Does the Resident Portal replace New Innovations or the Hub? ▾

No. The Resident Portal is provided for convenience. New Innovations and the Hub remain the official scheduling systems.

Who do I contact for urgent Pull List or Cross-Coverage issues? ▾

Please contact the On-Call Chief. See the Hub or the weekly Chief Resident email for the current On-Call Chief.

Master Schedule
Full rotation schedule with pull and XC markers. Download your personal calendar file below.

📅 Deploy to Your Phone Calendar

Select your name to download a .ics calendar file with your full year — rotations, pull days, and XC assignments. Open the file on your phone to import everything into your calendar at once.

How to add to your calendar:
  1. Select your name above
  2. Tap Download My Calendar (.ics)
  3. The file downloads — open it from your Downloads folder
  4. Tap Add to Calendar when prompted
  5. ✅ Your full year schedule imports automatically
Compatible with iPhone Calendar, Google Calendar, and Outlook. If the file does not open automatically, locate it in your Downloads folder and tap to open.

Swaps & Requests
Submit a form or contact the right chief. Please use individual UM emails.

🔄 Pull List Swap

Swaps must be one-to-one and submitted at least 6 weeks in advance. All swaps require chief approval.

Submit →

🔄 Cross-Coverage Swap

XC swaps must also be one-to-one and submitted in advance. Both residents must agree before submitting.

Submit →

⏰ Half-Day Clinic Request

Request a half-day clinic adjustment through the official form.

Submit →

🎯 Conference Coverage Request

Submit at least 45 days in advance. Reviewed by Dr. Brianna Mussman and Dr. Breno Bernardes.

Submit →
📧 Who Should I Contact?
Please use individual UM emails for all scheduling communication.

Jackson Scheduling, Pull List & Cross-Coverage

Dr. Shanice Spence-Miller
sss413@miami.edu
Dr. Mashli Fleurestil
m.fleurestil@miami.edu
Please copy both chiefs on scheduling emails.

VA Scheduling & Intern Year-Long Scheduling

Dr. Clara Oh
cjo108@miami.edu
Dr. Christian Guilliod
crg181@miami.edu
Please copy both chiefs on scheduling emails.

Conference & Clinic Scheduling

Dr. Breno Bernardes
breno.souza@med.miami.edu

Elective Swaps & Requests

Dr. Brianna Mussman
bmussman@med.miami.edu

🚨 On-Call Chief

Please see the Hub or the weekly Chief Resident email for the current On-Call Chief. Use the On-Call Chief only for urgent same-day or after-hours scheduling concerns.

Feedback
Help us make the portal better.
💬

We'd Love Your Feedback

This portal was built for you, and your input makes it better. If you have a suggestion, notice something that could be clearer, or simply want to share what's working well, we're listening. Every piece of feedback helps us make scheduling easier for the whole residency.

Share Your Thoughts →

What kind of feedback helps?

  • Something on the portal that's confusing or hard to find
  • A feature you wish existed
  • An error or assignment that doesn't look right
  • Anything that's working well and you'd like us to keep

For scheduling changes or swap requests, please use the Swaps & Requests tab instead. This form is for portal feedback.

Resident Portal Design by Dr. Shanice Spence-Miller
Internal Medicine Resident Portal • AY 2026–2027 • UM/JMH Internal Medicine Residency
